_doc: Falsification recipes for Odoo performance claims — used by realdata_test MCP (eval_orm_expression + consistency_check_eval) to verify perf claims mechanically. Each recipe encodes: (1) the claim shape, (2) a one-line code/data perturbation (PERTURB-TEST sentinel for grep revert), (3) the measurement command/tool hint, (4) the predicted delta with-vs-without the fix, (5) numeric tolerance, (6) a grep guard so revert can be verified post-run. Stack-detection: presence of `odoo-bin` / `__manifest__.py` in workspace. Schema matches the inverse-perturbation pattern in templates/cursor/skills/_common/claim-falsification/SKILL.md.

injection.notes: {related_field} must be a many2one declared on the model; if loop already accesses the chain naturally, skip injection and just enable query log.

measurement.command: self.env.cr.sql_log = True; <run loop body>; self.env.cr.sql_log = False
measurement.alt_command: with self.env.registry.cursor() as cr: cr.execute('SET log_statement = all'); <run>

predicted_delta.without_fix: query_count >= N  # one SELECT per record (N+1)

patterns.revert_check: grep -R 'PERTURB-TEST n_plus_one' addons/ && echo FAIL || echo OK

injection.notes: Branching + recordset rebinding mid-loop drops the prefetch cohort. If the original loop is already branchful, perturb by replacing first access with a `.sudo()` round-trip which also breaks prefetch.

measurement.command: before = len(self.env.cr.sql_log_count or []); <run loop>; after = len(self.env.cr.sql_log_count or [])

predicted_delta.with_fix: query_count stable at ~2 regardless of recordset size

patterns.revert_check: grep -R 'PERTURB-TEST prefetch_break' addons/ && echo FAIL || echo OK

injection.notes: Inject sleep(2) at the top of the compute method body. If the claim is that the compute is NOT in the write() dependency chain, then a write() to an unrelated field should NOT trigger the sleep.

measurement.command: t0=time.monotonic(); rec.write({'<unrelated_field>': new_value}); dt = time.monotonic() - t0

predicted_delta.with_fix: dt < 0.3  # compute not triggered by unrelated write
predicted_delta.without_fix: dt >= 2.0  # compute IS in dependency chain, picks up the sleep

patterns.revert_check: grep -R 'PERTURB-TEST stored_compute' addons/ && echo FAIL || echo OK

injection.inject_lines: self.env['<model>'].create([{{'<field>': 'val_%d' % i for i in range(10000)}} for i in range(10000)])  # PERTURB-TEST missing_index
injection.notes: Load 10k synthetic rows on dev DB only. NEVER run on production. After load, run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the same domain the claim references.

measurement.command: EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT id FROM <model>_table WHERE <field> = '<value>'
measurement.extract: first node type in plan: 'Seq Scan' vs 'Index Scan' / 'Index Only Scan' / 'Bitmap Index Scan'

predicted_delta.with_fix: plan contains 'Index Scan' or 'Index Only Scan' on <field>_index

patterns.revert_check: self.env['<model>'].search([('<field>', 'like', 'val_%')]).unlink(); grep -R 'PERTURB-TEST missing_index' addons/ && echo FAIL || echo OK

injection.inject_lines: # PERTURB-TEST sudo_count: no code change — measure existing sudo() call sites
injection.notes: Static count via grep; runtime measurement via stack inspection. Sudo bypasses ir.rule SQL JOIN but loses audit trail.

measurement.command: grep -c '\.sudo()' <module>/models/*.py; AND query_count_delta = run_with_sudo_disabled vs run_with_sudo

predicted_delta.with_fix: sudo_call_sites in hot path == 0, OR query_count_diff < 5%  # rules genuinely don't apply
predicted_delta.without_fix: sudo_call_sites > 0 in loop body AND query_count_without_sudo > query_count_with_sudo * 1.3

patterns.revert_check: no code change — confirm by `grep -R 'PERTURB-TEST sudo_count' addons/ && echo FAIL || echo OK`

injection.inject_lines: # PERTURB-TEST view_inheritance: no XML change — count inheriting views via ORM
injection.notes: Count inheriting views: self.env['ir.ui.view'].search([('inherit_id', '=', view_id)]) recursively. Then time fields_view_get / get_view.

measurement.command: view = self.env.ref('<xml_id>'); count = len(view._get_inheriting_views_arch(view.id, view.model)); t0=time.monotonic(); view.read_combined(['arch']); dt=time.monotonic()-t0

predicted_delta.with_fix: dt grows sub-linearly with count  # cache hits or shallow inheritance
predicted_delta.without_fix: dt grows linearly: dt_ms / count > 5ms per inheriting view

patterns.revert_check: no code change — confirm by `grep -R 'PERTURB-TEST view_inheritance' addons/ && echo FAIL || echo OK`

injection.inject_lines: # PERTURB-TEST cron_lock: schedule the same cron job to fire twice within 1 interval
injection.notes: Trigger the cron method directly from two separate cursors / two workers. Measure pg_locks for ir.cron row + the wall-clock wait of the 2nd run.

measurement.command: SELECT pid, mode, granted FROM pg_locks WHERE relation = 'ir_cron'::regclass; AND wall-clock of 2nd job

predicted_delta.with_fix: lock_wait_seconds < 0.5 OR 2nd job exits cleanly (skip-if-busy pattern)
predicted_delta.without_fix: lock_wait_seconds >= job_duration  # 2nd worker blocked on row lock until 1st finishes

patterns.revert_check: no code change — confirm by `grep -R 'PERTURB-TEST cron_lock' addons/ && echo FAIL || echo OK`

injection.notes: clear_caches() forces recompile of the QWeb template. Measure first render (cold) vs subsequent render (warm).

predicted_delta.with_fix: dt_cold / dt_warm < 3.0  # compile cost amortized or eagerly warmed
predicted_delta.without_fix: dt_cold / dt_warm >= 5.0  # cold render 5-10x slower than warm

patterns.revert_check: self.env['ir.qweb'].clear_caches() once more to re-stabilize; grep -R 'PERTURB-TEST qweb_cold_cache' addons/ && echo FAIL || echo OK

injection.inject_lines: # PERTURB-TEST mail_n_plus_one: count mail.message INSERTs while posting to 100 records
injection.notes: Post the same body to 100 records, count INSERT INTO mail_message statements. Single batched INSERT vs 100 single-row INSERTs.

measurement.command: with self.env.cr.savepoint(): self.env.cr.sql_log = True; for r in recs_100: r.message_post(body='x'); inserts = count_matching('INSERT INTO mail_message', self.env.cr.sql_log); self.env.cr.sql_log = False

predicted_delta.with_fix: inserts <= 5  # batched via execute_values / single INSERT
predicted_delta.without_fix: inserts >= 100  # one INSERT per record (true N+1 for mail)

patterns.revert_check:
self.env['mail.message'].search([('body', '=', '

x

')]).unlink(); grep -R 'PERTURB-TEST mail_n_plus_one' addons/ && echo FAIL || echo OK

injection.inject_lines: # PERTURB-TEST search_count_vs_len: dual call on same domain — no code change, parallel measurement
injection.notes: Run BOTH `len(Model.search(domain))` and `Model.search_count(domain)` on the same large table. Compare query plans + wall time.

measurement.command: t0=time.monotonic(); n1 = len(Model.search(domain)); dt_search = time.monotonic()-t0; t1=time.monotonic(); n2 = Model.search_count(domain); dt_count = time.monotonic()-t1; assert n1 == n2

predicted_delta.with_fix: dt_count < dt_search * 0.5 AND plan_count uses 'Aggregate' + 'COUNT(*)' without materializing rows
predicted_delta.without_fix: dt_count >= dt_search * 0.9  # claim is wrong — both plans equivalent on this domain

patterns.revert_check: no code change — confirm by `grep -R 'PERTURB-TEST search_count_vs_len' addons/ && echo FAIL || echo OK`
